summary: Blog post by Carole Herder promoting Best of America by Horseback (BOABH), a national trail-riding TV show, and showing Cavallo all-terrain hoof boots in action on the trail.
Short blog post sharing a customer photo of Barbara McPhillips carriage-driving in Cavallo Sport hoof boots, with a shout-out to UK equestrian retailer Horse and More.
Carole Herder opinion piece on the welfare of domestic horses kept in captivity, arguing they need a sense of control and freedom over their environment for better health and lifespan.
Carole Herder essay on horse shelter and stabling, arguing horses need basic dry shelter and socialization rather than being locked in stalls or over-protected like porcelain dolls.
Blog post by Carole Herder recounting a visit from the RCMP Musical Ride team to Cavallo, trialing Cavallo Comfort System Saddle Pads, with background on the Musical Ride tradition.
Personal narrative by Joe Camp about his mare Mariah suddenly going lame from a hoof abscess and his struggle to keep faith in natural, barefoot horse-care methods.
Carole Herder article questioning the over-use of horse blankets, explaining how blanketing can suppress a horse's natural thermoregulation and immune function.
Carole Herder post on natural/holistic equine health care, highlighting Dr. Robert Bowker's Corona Equine Vista Center and its hoof-care and lameness research.
Carole Herder article warning about pharmaceutical and toxin contamination in horse drinking water and the importance of clean hydration, especially in hot summer.
Carole Herder overview of recreational trail-riding sports ACTHA (USA) and TREC (Europe), describing their obstacle and horsemanship formats and how to get involved.
Carole Herder storytelling piece reflecting on farriers and the tradition of metal horseshoes versus the barefoot/trimming movement, framed around an encounter at Equine Affaire.
Customer thank-you note that turns into a list of the benefits of keeping horses barefoot, including healthier hooves, better circulation, fewer lameness issues, and lower farrier costs.
